message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation. Summary: 1. Primary Request and Intent: - User provided a comprehensive plan to build a full-stack todo list app in MoonBit with Rabbita (Elm-architecture frontend, JS target), Mocket (HTTP server backend, native target), and SQLite3 (database) - Frontend communicates with backend via REST API over JSON - User later asked for a Makefile to run the server, and to start the server - User reported the app "shows nothing" in the browser, triggering debugging 2. Key Technical Concepts: - **MoonBit language**: Structs, enums, traits, method chaining (`..` cascade operator), `#|` multiline strings, `derive(FromJson, ToJson)`, `try/catch`, `guard`, pattern matching on Json values - **Rabbita**: `cell_with_dispatch` for stateful cells with side effects, `Dispatch[Msg]`, `Cmd`, `Html` elements (div, h1, form, input, button, ul, li, span), `@http.get/post/patch/delete` for HTTP requests, `Expecting::Json` and `Expecting::Text`, `App::new().with_init().mount()` - **Mocket**: `Mocket::new()`, route registration (`.get()`, `.post()`, `.delete()`), `HttpResponse::new(StatusCode).json()/.body()`, `MocketEvent` (req, res, params), `@cors.handle_cors()` middleware, `Responder` trait, `static_assets()` - **SQLite3**: `Connection::open()`, `prepare()`, `bind()`/`bind_string_as_blob()`, `step()`/`step_once()`, `column()`/`column_blob_as_string()`, `finalize()`, `SELECT last_insert_rowid()` - **Build system**: `moon.mod.json` for deps, `moon.pkg` for package config with `supported-targets`, `moon build --target js/native`, `moon run backend --target native` 3. Files and Code Sections: - **`/Users/haoxiang/Workspace/moonbit/feihaoxiang/rabbita-mocket-sqlite3-todo-list/moon.mod.json`** - Root project config with dependencies ```json { "name": "haoxiang/todo-list", "version": "0.1.0", "deps": { "moonbit-community/rabbita": "0.11.5", "oboard/mocket": "0.6.10", "myfreess/sqlite3": "0.1.1", "moonbitlang/x": "0.4.40" } } ``` - **`/Users/haoxiang/Workspace/moonbit/feihaoxiang/rabbita-mocket-sqlite3-todo-list/backend/moon.pkg`** - Backend package config (native target) ``` import { "oboard/mocket", "oboard/mocket/cors", "myfreess/sqlite3", "moonbitlang/core/json", "moonbitlang/core/strconv", "moonbitlang/x/fs", } options( "is-main": true, "supported-targets": ["native"], ) ``` - **`/Users/haoxiang/Workspace/moonbit/feihaoxiang/rabbita-mocket-sqlite3-todo-list/backend/main.mbt`** - Backend server with SQLite3 CRUD and Mocket HTTP routes - Most recent state (after last edit, before testing): - `serve_file` function exists but is unused - Root route "/" serves inline HTML via `HttpResponse::new(OK, headers=..., raw_body=@utf8.encode(html))` — **UNTESTED** - `/frontend.js` route sets `res.raw_body = bytes` directly — **UNTESTED** - API routes: `GET /api/todos`, `POST /api/todos`, `POST /api/todos/:id/toggle`, `DELETE /api/todos/:id` — all verified working - Key current code for the root route (last edit): ```moonbit ..get("/", fn(_event) noraise { let html = #| #| #| #| #| #| Todo List #| #| #| #|

content.text: Both routes work. The app should now render properly. Let me also quickly test the API.

Everything is working. The fix was:

1. **Root cause**: `@utf8.encode()` was used to convert HTML string to bytes for `raw_body`, but the `utf8` package wasn't imported.
2. **Fix**: Added `"moonbitlang/core/encoding/utf8"` to the backend's `moon.pkg` imports and set `raw_body=@utf8.encode(html)` directly in the `HttpResponse::new()` constructor.

The server is running at **http://localhost:4000** — you should now see the Todo List app with the input field and Add button. Try adding some todos!

1. Commands that will be commonly used, such as how to build, lint, and run tests. Include the necessary commands to develop in this codebase, such as how to run a single test.

Usage notes:
- If there's already a CLAUDE.md, suggest improvements to it.
- When you make the initial CLAUDE.md, do not repeat yourself and do not include obvious instructions like "Provide helpful error messages to users", "Write unit tests for all new utilities", "Never include sensitive information (API keys, tokens) in code or commits".
- Avoid listing every component or file structure that can be easily discovered.
- Don't include generic development practices.
- If there are Cursor rules (in .cursor/rules/ or .cursorrules) or Copilot rules (in .github/copilot-instructions.md), make sure to include the important parts.
- If there is a README.md, make sure to include the important parts.

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
```

107→ #| 108→ #| 109→ #| 110→ #| 111→ #| 112→ #| Todo List 113→ #| 117→ #| 118→ #| 119→ #|
120→ #| 121→ #| 122→ #| 123→ @mocket.HttpResponse::new( 124→ OK, 125→ headers={ "Content-Type": "text/html; charset=utf-8" }, 126→ raw_body=@utf8.encode(html), 127→ ) 128→ }) 129→ ..get("/frontend.js", fn(_event) noraise { 130→ try { 131→ let bytes = @fs.read_file_to_bytes("./public/frontend.js") 132→ let res = @mocket.HttpResponse::new(OK) 133→ res.headers["Content-Type"] = "application/javascript; charset=utf-8" 134→ res.raw_body = bytes 135→ res 136→ } catch { 137→ _ => @mocket.HttpResponse::new(NotFound).body("Not Found") 138→ } 139→ }) 140→ ..get("/api/todos", fn(_event) noraise { 141→ try { 142→ let todos = get_all_todos(conn) 143→ @mocket.HttpResponse::new(OK).json(todos) 144→ } catch { 145→ e => @mocket.HttpResponse::new(InternalServerError).body("\{e}") 146→ } 147→ }) 148→ ..post("/api/todos", fn(event) noraise { 149→ try { 150→ let json : Json = event.req.body() 151→ let text = match json { 152→ { "text": String(t), .. } => t 153→ _ => { 154→ return @mocket.HttpResponse::new(BadRequest).body( 155→ "Missing text field", 156→ ) 157→ } 158→ } 159→ let todo = create_todo(conn, text) 160→ @mocket.HttpResponse::new(Created).json(todo) 161→ } catch { 162→ e => @mocket.HttpResponse::new(InternalServerError).body("\{e}") 163→ } 164→ }) 165→ ..post("/api/todos/:id/toggle", fn(event) noraise { 166→ try { 167→ let id_str = event.params.get("id").unwrap() 168→ let id = @strconv.parse_int(id_str.to_string()) 169→ let todo = toggle_todo(conn, id) 170→ @mocket.HttpResponse::new(OK).json(todo) 171→ } catch { 172→ e => @mocket.HttpResponse::new(InternalServerError).body("\{e}") 173→ } 174→ }) 175→ .delete("/api/todos/:id", fn(event) noraise { 176→ try { 177→ let id_str = event.params.get("id").unwrap() 178→ let id = @strconv.parse_int(id_str.to_string()) 179→ delete_todo(conn, id) 180→ @mocket.HttpResponse::new(NoContent) 181→ } catch { 182→ e => @mocket.HttpResponse::new(InternalServerError).body("\{e}") 183→ } 184→ }) 185→ println("Server running at http://localhost:4000") 186→ app.serve(port=4000) 187→} 188→

1→///|
     2→struct Todo {
     3→  id : Int
     4→  text : String
     5→  done : Bool
     6→} derive(FromJson)
     7→
     8→///|
     9→struct Model {
    10→  todos : Array[Todo]
    11→  input : String
    12→  loading : Bool
    13→}
    14→
    15→///|
    16→enum Msg {
    17→  Init
    18→  GotTodos(Result[Array[Todo], String])
    19→  InputChanged(String)
    20→  AddTodo
    21→  TodoAdded(Result[Todo, String])
    22→  ToggleTodo(Int)
    23→  TodoToggled(Result[Todo, String])
    24→  DeleteTodo(Int)
    25→  TodoDeleted(Result[Int, String])
    26→}
    27→
    28→///|
    29→fn fetch_todos(dispatch : @rabbita.Dispatch[Msg]) -> @rabbita.Cmd {
    30→  @http.get(
    31→    "/api/todos",
    32→    expect=Json(
    33→      fn(result) { dispatch(GotTodos(result)) },
    34→      fn(json) {
    35→        try {
    36→          Ok(@json.from_json(json))
    37→        } catch {
    38→          _ => Err("Failed to decode todos")
    39→        }
    40→      },
    41→    ),
    42→  )
    43→}
    44→
    45→///|
    46→fn update(
    47→  dispatch : @rabbita.Dispatch[Msg],
    48→  msg : Msg,
    49→  model : Model,
    50→) -> (@rabbita.Cmd, Model) {
    51→  match msg {
    52→    Init => (fetch_todos(dispatch), { ..model, loading: true })
    53→    GotTodos(Ok(todos)) => (@rabbita.none, { ..model, todos, loading: false })
    54→    GotTodos(Err(_)) => (@rabbita.none, { ..model, loading: false })
    55→    InputChanged(value) => (@rabbita.none, { ..model, input: value })
    56→    AddTodo => {
    57→      if model.input == "" {
    58→        return (@rabbita.none, model)
    59→      }
    60→      let text = model.input
    61→      let cmd = @http.post(
    62→        "/api/todos",
    63→        Json(({ "text": text } : Json)),
    64→        expect=Json(
    65→          fn(result) { dispatch(TodoAdded(result)) },
    66→          fn(json) {
    67→            try {
    68→              Ok(@json.from_json(json))
    69→            } catch {
    70→              _ => Err("Failed to decode todo")
    71→            }
    72→          },
    73→        ),
    74→      )
    75→      (cmd, { ..model, input: "" })
    76→    }
    77→    TodoAdded(Ok(todo)) => {
    78→      let todos = model.todos.copy()
    79→      todos.push(todo)
    80→      (@rabbita.none, { ..model, todos })
    81→    }
    82→    TodoAdded(Err(_)) => (@rabbita.none, model)
    83→    ToggleTodo(id) => {
    84→      let cmd = @http.post(
    85→        "/api/todos/\{id}/toggle",
    86→        Json(({} : Json)),
    87→        expect=Json(
    88→          fn(result) { dispatch(TodoToggled(result)) },
    89→          fn(json) {
    90→            try {
    91→              Ok(@json.from_json(json))
    92→            } catch {
    93→              _ => Err("Failed to decode todo")
    94→            }
    95→          },
    96→        ),
    97→      )
    98→      (cmd, model)
    99→    }
   100→    TodoToggled(Ok(updated)) => {
   101→      let todos = model.todos.map(
   102→        fn(t) { if t.id == updated.id { updated } else { t } },
   103→      )
   104→      (@rabbita.none, { ..model, todos })
   105→    }
   106→    TodoToggled(Err(_)) => (@rabbita.none, model)
   107→    DeleteTodo(id) => {
   108→      let cmd = @http.delete(
   109→        "/api/todos/\{id}",
   110→        expect=Text(
   111→          fn(result) {
   112→            dispatch(
   113→              TodoDeleted(
   114→                match result {
   115→                  Ok(_) => Ok(id)
   116→                  Err(e) => Err(e)
   117→                },
   118→              ),
   119→            )
   120→          },
   121→        ),
   122→      )
   123→      (cmd, model)
   124→    }
   125→    TodoDeleted(Ok(id)) => {
   126→      let todos = model.todos.filter(fn(t) { t.id != id })
   127→      (@rabbita.none, { ..model, todos })
   128→    }
   129→    TodoDeleted(Err(_)) => (@rabbita.none, model)
   130→  }
   131→}
   132→
   133→///|
   134→fn view(dispatch : @rabbita.Dispatch[Msg], model : Model) -> @rabbita.Html {
   135→  @html.div(
   136→    style=["max-width: 500px", "margin: 40px auto", "padding: 20px",
   137→      "background: white", "border-radius: 8px", "box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.1)"],
   138→    [
   139→      @html.h1(
   140→        style=["text-align: center", "margin-bottom: 20px", "color: #2c3e50"],
   141→        "Todo List",
   142→      ),
   143→      @html.form(
   144→        on_submit=dispatch(AddTodo),
   145→        style=["display: flex", "gap: 8px", "margin-bottom: 20px"],
   146→        [
   147→          @html.input(
   148→            input_type=Text,
   149→            placeholder="What needs to be done?",
   150→            value=model.input,
   151→            on_input=fn(value) { dispatch(InputChanged(value)) },
   152→            style=["flex: 1", "padding: 8px 12px", "border: 1px solid #ddd",
   153→              "border-radius: 4px", "font-size: 14px", "outline: none"],
   154→            "",
   155→          ),
   156→          @html.button(
   157→            type_="submit",
   158→            style=["padding: 8px 16px", "background: #3498db", "color: white",
   159→              "border: none", "border-radius: 4px", "cursor: pointer", "font-size: 14px"],
   160→            "Add",
   161→          ),
   162→        ],
   163→      ),
   164→      if model.loading {
   165→        @html.p(style=["text-align: center", "color: #999"], "Loading...")
   166→      } else if model.todos.length() == 0 {
   167→        @html.p(
   168→          style=["text-align: center", "color: #999", "padding: 20px"],
   169→          "No todos yet. Add one above!",
   170→        )
   171→      } else {
   172→        @html.ul(
   173→          style=["list-style: none"],
   174→          model.todos.map(
   175→            fn(todo) {
   176→              @html.li(
   177→                style=["display: flex", "align-items: center", "padding: 10px 0",
   178→                  "border-bottom: 1px solid #eee"],
   179→                [
   180→                  @html.input(
   181→                    input_type=Checkbox,
   182→                    checked=todo.done,
   183→                    on_change=fn(_value) {
   184→                      dispatch(ToggleTodo(todo.id))
   185→                    },
   186→                    style=["margin-right: 12px", "cursor: pointer"],
   187→                    "",
   188→                  ),
   189→                  @html.span(
   190→                    style=if todo.done {
   191→                      ["flex: 1", "text-decoration: line-through", "color: #999"]
   192→                    } else {
   193→                      ["flex: 1"]
   194→                    },
   195→                    todo.text,
   196→                  ),
   197→                  @html.button(
   198→                    on_click=dispatch(DeleteTodo(todo.id)),
   199→                    style=["padding: 4px 8px", "background: #e74c3c", "color: white",
   200→                      "border: none", "border-radius: 4px", "cursor: pointer",
   201→                      "font-size: 12px"],
   202→                    "Delete",
   203→                  ),
   204→                ],
   205→              )
   206→            },
   207→          ),
   208→        )
   209→      },
   210→    ],
   211→  )
   212→}
   213→
   214→///|
   215→fn main {
   216→  let (dispatch, app_cell) = @rabbita.cell_with_dispatch(
   217→    model={ todos: [], input: "", loading: false },
   218→    update~,
   219→    view~,
   220→  )
   221→  let app = @rabbita.new(app_cell)
   222→  app.with_init(dispatch(Init))
   223→  app.mount("app")
   224→}
   225→

## Project Analysis Report: MoonBit Todo List Application

```json

    "oboard/mocket": "0.6.10",                   // HTTP server

  }
}
```

- `make build-backend`: Compiles MoonBit to native binary (targets native)
- `make build`: Builds both frontend and backend
- `make serve`: Builds everything and runs the backend server on localhost:4000
- `make clean`: Removes build artifacts and database

- Frontend: `_build/js/debug/build/frontend/frontend.js`
- Backend: `_build/native/debug/build/backend/` (executable binary)

### 4. **Package Configuration**

**frontend/moon.pkg**:
```
Imports:
- moonbit-community/rabbita (main framework)
- moonbit-community/rabbita/html (HTML DSL)
- moonbit-community/rabbita/http (HTTP client)
- moonbitlang/core/json (JSON parsing)

Options:
- is-main: true
- supported-targets: ["js"]
```

**backend/moon.pkg**:
```
Imports:
- oboard/mocket (HTTP server framework)
- oboard/mocket/cors (CORS middleware)
- myfreess/sqlite3 (SQLite bindings)
- moonbitlang/core/json (JSON serialization)
- moonbitlang/core/strconv (String conversion, for URL params)
- moonbitlang/core/encoding/utf8 (UTF-8 handling)
- moonbitlang/x/fs (File system operations)

Options:
- is-main: true
- supported-targets: ["native"]
```

### 5. **Key Architectural Patterns**

#### **Frontend-Backend Communication**

The architecture follows a **REST API pattern with JSON**:

**Frontend (Rabbita framework)**:
- **Model-Update-View (MVU)** architecture
- State management via `Model` struct containing todos and input
- HTTP client library for API calls
- Commands (`@rabbita.Cmd`) for async operations

**API Endpoints**:
- `GET /` - Serves HTML page with embedded frontend.js
- `GET /api/todos` - Fetches all todos as JSON array
- `POST /api/todos` - Creates new todo (expects `{"text": "..."}`)
- `POST /api/todos/:id/toggle` - Toggle done status
- `DELETE /api/todos/:id` - Delete todo
- `GET /frontend.js` - Serves compiled frontend JavaScript

**Data Flow**:
1. Frontend initializes, dispatches `Init` message
2. `fetch_todos()` command makes GET request to `/api/todos`
3. Backend queries SQLite, returns JSON array
4. Frontend updates model with todos and renders
5. User interactions (add/toggle/delete) dispatch messages
6. HTTP commands send requests, receive JSON responses
7. Model updates trigger re-render

**Backend (Mocket framework)**:
- HTTP server on port 4000
- CORS middleware enabled for cross-origin requests
- SQLite3 database persistence
- Error handling with try-catch blocks

#### **Database Schema**

```sql
CREATE TABLE todos (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
  text BLOB NOT NULL,
  done INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0
)
```

Text is stored as BLOB, done is INTEGER (0=false, 1=true).

Frontend Todo struct:

struct Todo {
  id : Int
  text : String
  done : Bool
} derive(FromJson)
```

The `FromJson` derivation enables automatic JSON deserialization.

- Rabbita framework tests (async, object, value, HTML, URL)
- Mocket server tests (utils, cookies, object, value)
- MoonBit standard library tests (crypto, encoding, fs, json5, time, uuid, etc.)

These are not part of the application code and are ignored in `.gitignore`.

### 7. **Key Implementation Details**

**Frontend Features**:
- Real-time input field for adding todos
- Checkbox toggle for completion status
- Delete button per todo
- Loading state display
- Empty state message
- Inline styling with CSS arrays
- Error handling for API calls

**Backend Features**:
- Database initialization on startup
- Automatic ID generation with `last_insert_rowid()`
- Blob storage for todo text (handles Unicode)
- CORS middleware for browser requests
- Static HTML page with embedded script tag
- UTF-8 encoding for HTTP responses
- File serving for compiled frontend.js

**Entry Points**:
- Frontend: `frontend/main.mbt` → `main()` function mounts to DOM element "app"
- Backend: `backend/main.mbt` → `main()` function initializes DB and starts server

### 8. **Dependencies at a Glance**

| Dependency | Version | Purpose | Used By |
|---|---|---|---|
| rabbita | 0.11.5 | Frontend MVU framework & HTML DSL | Frontend |
| mocket | 0.6.10 | HTTP server framework | Backend |
| sqlite3 | 0.1.1 | SQLite database bindings | Backend |
| moonbitlang/x | 0.4.40 | Standard library extensions (fs, strconv, encoding) | Both |

### 9. **Development Workflow**

1. **Edit** `frontend/main.mbt` or `backend/main.mbt`
2. **Run** `make serve` (full rebuild + run)
3. **Access** `http://localhost:4000`
4. **Database** persists in `todos.db`
5. **Clean** with `make clean` (removes db and frontend.js)

### 10. **Notable Code Patterns**

- **Error handling**: Extensive use of `raise` and `try-catch` for database errors
- **JSON conversion**: `@json.from_json()` for deserialization, Json literal syntax for serialization
- **String binding**: Blob columns use `.bind_string_as_blob()` and `.column_blob_as_string()` for UTF-8 safety
- **Statement preparation**: All SQL uses prepared statements with `.bind()` for parameter injection
- **HTML DSL**: Type-safe HTML generation with function calls and style arrays
- **Event dispatch**: Frontend uses functional dispatch pattern with message passing

---

This is a clean, minimal full-stack MoonBit application demonstrating modern web framework patterns (MVU on frontend, middleware-based HTTP on backend) with persistent SQLite storage. The code is well-structured with clear separation of concerns and comprehensive error handling.

input.content:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Build & Run

```bash
make serve          # Build frontend (js) + backend (native), then run server on :4000
make build          # Build both targets without running
make build-frontend # moon build --target js, copy JS to public/
make build-backend  # moon build --target native
make clean          # Remove public/frontend.js, todos.db, and _build/
```

The server runs at http://localhost:4000. Data persists in `todos.db` (SQLite).

## Architecture

Full-stack MoonBit todo app with two separate compilation targets in one module:

- **Frontend** (`frontend/main.mbt`, target: `js`) — Rabbita framework using Model-Update-View (Elm architecture). Compiles to JS, served by backend at `/frontend.js`.
- **Backend** (`backend/main.mbt`, target: `native`) — Mocket HTTP server with SQLite3 persistence. Serves HTML inline, the compiled frontend JS from `public/frontend.js`, and a REST API.

Frontend and backend communicate via JSON REST API:
- `GET /api/todos` — list all
- `POST /api/todos` — create (`{"text": "..."}`)
- `POST /api/todos/:id/toggle` — toggle done status
- `DELETE /api/todos/:id` — delete

## Key Dependencies

| Package | Version | Target | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `moonbit-community/rabbita` | 0.11.5 | js | MVU frontend framework + HTML DSL + HTTP client |
| `oboard/mocket` | 0.6.10 | native | HTTP server framework with CORS middleware |
| `myfreess/sqlite3` | 0.1.1 | native | SQLite3 bindings |
| `moonbitlang/x` | 0.4.40 | native | Filesystem (`@fs`) |

## MoonBit Patterns Used

- **SQLite text columns**: Use `bind_string_as_blob` / `column_blob_as_string` (not `bind_string` / `column`) for UTF-8 safety — the schema stores `text` as `BLOB`.
- **Mocket response bodies**: `HttpResponse::new(OK).json(data)` works reliably. For non-JSON responses, set `raw_body=@utf8.encode(str)` directly in the constructor — the `.body(&Responder)` method doesn't work for strings in native target.
- **Rabbita HTTP**: No `PUT`/`PATCH` — use `@http.post` / `@http.delete` / `@http.get`. Mocket's native server only reads request body for `POST`, so toggle uses `POST` instead of `PATCH`.
- **Rabbita dispatch**: Use `cell_with_dispatch` (not `simple_cell`) when the update function needs to issue HTTP commands.

input.content:
# Todo List

A full-stack todo list application written entirely in [MoonBit](https://www.moonbitlang.com/).

- **Frontend**: [Rabbita](https://github.com/moonbit-community/rabbita) (Elm-architecture UI framework, compiles to JS)
- **Backend**: [Mocket](https://github.com/oboard/mocket) (HTTP server, compiles to native) + [SQLite3](https://github.com/myfreess/sqlite3) (persistence)

## Quick Start

```bash
moon install
make serve
```

Open http://localhost:4000.

## Features

- Add, toggle, and delete todos
- Data persists in SQLite (`todos.db`)
- Single codebase, two compilation targets (`js` for frontend, `native` for backend)
- REST API with JSON communication between frontend and backend

## API

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `GET` | `/api/todos` | List all todos |
| `POST` | `/api/todos` | Create a todo (`{"text": "..."}`) |
| `POST` | `/api/todos/:id/toggle` | Toggle done status |
| `DELETE` | `/api/todos/:id` | Delete a todo |

## Project Structure

```
backend/main.mbt    # Mocket HTTP server + SQLite3 CRUD
frontend/main.mbt   # Rabbita MVU app (model, update, view)
public/             # Build output for frontend JS
moon.mod.json       # Module config and dependencies
Makefile            # Build and run commands
```

    97→///|

    99→
   100→///|
   101→let ws_handler_map : Map[Int, WebSocketHandler] = Map::new()
   102→
   103→///|
   104→pub fn register_ws_handler(mocket : Mocket, port : Int) -> Unit {
   105→  let mut done = false
   106→  mocket.ws_static_routes.each(fn(_, handler) {
   107→    if not(done) {
   108→      ws_handler_map.set(port, handler)
   109→      done = true
   110→    }
   111→  })
   112→}
   113→
   114→///|
   115→pub fn serve_ffi(mocket : Mocket, port~ : Int) -> Unit {
   116→  server_map[port] = mocket
   117→  register_ws_handler(mocket, port)
   118→  set_ws_emit(fn(
   119→    event_type : @native.CStr,
   120→    id : @native.CStr,
   121→    payload : @native.CStr,
   122→  ) {
   123→    __ws_emit(event_type, id, payload)
   124→  })
   125→  let server = create_server(fn(
   126→    port : Int,
   127→    req : HttpRequestInternal,
   128→    res : HttpResponseInternal,
   129→  ) {
   130→    handle_request_native(port, req, res)
   131→  })
   132→  server_listen(server, port)
   133→}
   134→
   135→///|
   136→fn handle_request_native(
   137→  port : Int,
   138→  req : HttpRequestInternal,
   139→  res : HttpResponseInternal,
   140→) -> Unit {
   141→  let mocket = server_map[port]
   142→  // 直接使用 C FFI 获取请求数据
   143→  let url = from_cstr(req.url())
   144→  let path = url
   145→  let http_method = from_cstr(req.req_method())
   146→
   147→  // // 创建 HttpRequest
   148→  // let http_req : HttpRequest = {
   149→  //   http_method: http_method,
   150→  //   url,
   151→  //   body,
   152→  //   headers: Map::new(),
   153→  // }
   154→  // let http_res = { status_code: 200, headers: Map::new() }
   155→
   156→  // // 创建 HttpEvent
   157→  // let event : HttpEvent = { req: http_req, res: http_res, params: Map::new() }
   158→
   159→  // // 简单响应
   160→  // res.set_header(to_cstr("Content-Type"), to_cstr("application/json"))
   161→  // res.status(200)
   162→  // res.end(to_cstr("{\"status\":\"ok\"}"))
   163→
   164→  let headers_str = from_cstr(req.headers())
   165→  let req_headers = Map::new()
   166→  headers_str
   167→  .split("\n")
   168→  .each(fn(line) {
   169→    let line = line.trim()
   170→    if line == "" {
   171→      return
   172→    }
   173→    try {
   174→      if line.find(":") is Some(idx) {
   175→        let key = line[0:idx].trim()
   176→        let value = line[idx + 1:].trim()
   177→        if key != "" {
   178→          req_headers.set(key, value)
   179→        }
   180→      }
   181→    } catch {
   182→      _ => ()
   183→    }
   184→  })
   185→  let (params, handler) = match mocket.find_route(http_method, path) {
   186→    Some((h, p)) => (p, h)
   187→    _ => ({}, handle_not_found())
   188→  }
   189→  let event = {
   190→    req: { http_method, url, raw_body: "", headers: req_headers },
   191→    res: HttpResponse::new(OK),
   192→    params,
   193→  }
   194→  if http_method == "POST" {
   195→    let req_body_len = req.req_body_len()
   196→    let req_body = req.body()
   197→    let safe_body_len = if req_body_len < 0 {
   198→      0
   199→    } else if req_body_len > req_body.length() {
   200→      req_body.length()
   201→    } else {
   202→      req_body_len
   203→    }
   204→    let body_bytes = req_body[0:safe_body_len]
   205→    event.req.raw_body = body_bytes.to_bytes()
   206→  }
   207→  async_run(async fn() noraise {
   208→    // 执行中间件链和处理器
   209→    let responder = execute_middlewares(mocket.middlewares, event, handler)
   210→    responder.options(event.res)
   211→    res.status(event.res.status_code.to_int())
   212→    event.res.headers.each((key, value) => {
   213→      res.set_header(to_cstr(key), to_cstr(value))
   214→    })
   215→    event.res.cookies
   216→    .values()
   217→    .each(cookie => {
   218→      res.set_header(to_cstr("Set-Cookie"), to_cstr(cookie.to_string()))
   219→    })
   220→    let buf = @buffer.new()
   221→    responder.output(buf)
   222→    event.res.raw_body = buf.to_bytes()
   223→    res.end_bytes(event.res.raw_body)
   224→  })
   225→}
   226→
   227→///|
   228→pub fn __ws_emit(
   229→  event_type : @native.CStr,
   230→  connection_id : @native.CStr,
   231→  payload : @native.CStr,
   232→) -> Unit {
   233→  let et = from_cstr(event_type)
   234→  let cid = from_cstr(connection_id)
   235→  let handler = if ws_handler_map.is_empty() {
   236→    fn(_) {  }
   237→  } else {
   238→    ws_handler_map.get(ws_handler_map.keys().collect()[0]).unwrap()
   239→  }
   240→  let peer = WebSocketPeer::{ connection_id: cid, subscribed_channels: [] }
   241→  match et {
   242→    "open" => handler(WebSocketEvent::Open(peer))
   243→    "message" => {
   244→      let pl = from_cstr(payload)
   245→      handler(WebSocketEvent::Message(peer, Text(pl)))
   246→    }
   247→    "binary" => {
   248→      let len = ws_msg_body_len()
   249→      let arr = Array::make(len, b'\x00')
   250→      let buf = Bytes::from_array(arr)
   251→      let copied = ws_msg_copy(buf)
   252→      let body = buf[0:copied].to_bytes()
   253→      handler(WebSocketEvent::Message(peer, Binary(body)))
   254→    }
   255→    "ping" => handler(WebSocketEvent::Message(peer, Ping))
   256→    "close" => handler(WebSocketEvent::Close(peer))
   257→    _ => ()
   258→  }
   259→}
   260→
   261→///|
   262→#owned(id, msg)
   263→extern "c" fn ws_send_native(id : @native.CStr, msg : @native.CStr) -> Unit = "ws_send"
   264→

content.text:
Build succeeded. The issue is in the native server code. Looking at `handle_request_native` (lines 207-223), the server calls `responder.output(buf)` on the **returned responder** — when our handler returns an `HttpResponse`, the `Responder for HttpResponse` implementation writes `self.raw_body` to the buffer.

So `raw_body` set in the constructor should work... unless the `Bytes` from `@fs.read_file_to_bytes` is somehow empty. Let me test.

content.text:
All three routes work after `make clean && make serve`:
- `/` — HTML, 452 bytes
- `/frontend.js` — JS, 562564 bytes  
- `/api/todos` — JSON `[]`

The app works correctly. The issue you experienced was likely a **timing problem** — after `make clean`, the build takes some time, and if you accessed the page before the server was fully started, you'd see nothing. The server doesn't print its "ready" message until after the build completes and the native binary starts.

Try waiting ~10-15 seconds after `make serve` starts before opening the browser. You should see the Todo List app.

1→///| 2→fn init_db(conn : @sqlite3.Connection) -> Unit raise @sqlite3.SqliteError { 3→ let stmt = conn.prepare( 4→ ( 5→ #|CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS todos ( 6→ #| id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, 7→ #| text BLOB NOT NULL, 8→ #| done INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 9→ #|) 10→ ), 11→ ) 12→ stmt.step_once() 13→ stmt.finalize() 14→} 15→ 16→///| 17→fn todo_to_json(id : Int, text : String, done : Int) -> Json { 18→ { "id": id, "text": text, "done": done != 0 } 19→} 20→ 21→///| 22→fn get_all_todos( 23→ conn : @sqlite3.Connection, 24→) -> Array[Json] raise @sqlite3.SqliteError { 25→ let stmt = conn.prepare("SELECT id, text, done FROM todos ORDER BY id") 26→ let todos : Array[Json] = [] 27→ while stmt.step() { 28→ let id : Int = stmt.column(index=0) 29→ let text : String = stmt.column_blob_as_string(index=1) 30→ let done : Int = stmt.column(index=2) 31→ todos.push(todo_to_json(id, text, done)) 32→ } 33→ stmt.finalize() 34→ todos 35→} 36→ 37→///| 38→fn create_todo( 39→ conn : @sqlite3.Connection, 40→ text : String, 41→) -> Json raise @sqlite3.SqliteError { 42→ let stmt = conn.prepare("INSERT INTO todos (text, done) VALUES (?, 0)") 43→ stmt.bind_string_as_blob(index=1, val=text) 44→ stmt.step_once() 45→ stmt.finalize() 46→ let stmt2 = conn.prepare("SELECT last_insert_rowid()") 47→ guard stmt2.step() 48→ let id : Int = stmt2.column(index=0) 49→ stmt2.finalize() 50→ todo_to_json(id, text, 0) 51→} 52→ 53→///| 54→fn toggle_todo( 55→ conn : @sqlite3.Connection, 56→ id : Int, 57→) -> Json raise @sqlite3.SqliteError { 58→ let stmt = conn.prepare( 59→ "UPDATE todos SET done = CASE WHEN done = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END WHERE id = ?", 60→ ) 61→ stmt.bind(index=1, val=id) 62→ stmt.step_once() 63→ stmt.finalize() 64→ let stmt2 = conn.prepare("SELECT id, text, done FROM todos WHERE id = ?") 65→ stmt2.bind(index=1, val=id) 66→ guard stmt2.step() 67→ let text : String = stmt2.column_blob_as_string(index=1) 68→ let done_val : Int = stmt2.column(index=2) 69→ stmt2.finalize() 70→ todo_to_json(id, text, done_val) 71→} 72→ 73→///| 74→fn delete_todo( 75→ conn : @sqlite3.Connection, 76→ id : Int, 77→) -> Unit raise @sqlite3.SqliteError { 78→ let stmt = conn.prepare("DELETE FROM todos WHERE id = ?") 79→ stmt.bind(index=1, val=id) 80→ stmt.step_once() 81→ stmt.finalize() 82→} 83→ 84→///| 85→fn main { 86→ let conn = try { 87→ @sqlite3.Connection::open("todos.db") 88→ } catch { 89→ e => { 90→ println("Failed to open database: \{e}") 91→ return 92→ } 93→ } 94→ try { 95→ init_db(conn) 96→ } catch { 97→ e => { 98→ println("Failed to init database: \{e}") 99→ return 100→ } 101→ } 102→ let app = @mocket.new() 103→ app 104→ ..use_middleware(@cors.handle_cors()) 105→ ..get("/", fn(_event) noraise { 106→ let html = 107→ #| 108→ #| 109→ #| 110→ #| 111→ #| 112→ #| Todo List 113→ #| 117→ #| 118→ #| 119→ #|
120→ #| 121→ #| 122→ #| 123→ @mocket.HttpResponse::new( 124→ OK, 125→ headers={ "Content-Type": "text/html; charset=utf-8" }, 126→ raw_body=@utf8.encode(html), 127→ ) 128→ }) 129→ ..get("/frontend.js", fn(_event) noraise { 130→ try { 131→ let bytes = @fs.read_file_to_bytes("./public/frontend.js") 132→ @mocket.HttpResponse::new( 133→ OK, 134→ headers={ "Content-Type": "application/javascript; charset=utf-8" }, 135→ raw_body=bytes, 136→ ) 137→ } catch { 138→ _ => @mocket.HttpResponse::new(NotFound).body("Not Found") 139→ } 140→ }) 141→ ..get("/api/todos", fn(_event) noraise { 142→ try { 143→ let todos = get_all_todos(conn) 144→ @mocket.HttpResponse::new(OK).json(todos) 145→ } catch { 146→ e => @mocket.HttpResponse::new(InternalServerError).body("\{e}") 147→ } 148→ }) 149→ ..post("/api/todos", fn(event) noraise { 150→ try { 151→ let json : Json = event.req.body() 152→ let text = match json { 153→ { "text": String(t), .. } => t 154→ _ => { 155→ return @mocket.HttpResponse::new(BadRequest).body( 156→ "Missing text field", 157→ ) 158→ } 159→ } 160→ let todo = create_todo(conn, text) 161→ @mocket.HttpResponse::new(Created).json(todo) 162→ } catch { 163→ e => @mocket.HttpResponse::new(InternalServerError).body("\{e}") 164→ } 165→ }) 166→ ..post("/api/todos/:id/toggle", fn(event) noraise { 167→ try { 168→ let id_str = event.params.get("id").unwrap() 169→ let id = @strconv.parse_int(id_str.to_string()) 170→ let todo = toggle_todo(conn, id) 171→ @mocket.HttpResponse::new(OK).json(todo) 172→ } catch { 173→ e => @mocket.HttpResponse::new(InternalServerError).body("\{e}") 174→ } 175→ }) 176→ .delete("/api/todos/:id", fn(event) noraise { 177→ try { 178→ let id_str = event.params.get("id").unwrap() 179→ let id = @strconv.parse_int(id_str.to_string()) 180→ delete_todo(conn, id) 181→ @mocket.HttpResponse::new(NoContent) 182→ } catch { 183→ e => @mocket.HttpResponse::new(InternalServerError).body("\{e}") 184→ } 185→ }) 186→ println("Server running at http://localhost:4000") 187→ app.serve(port=4000) 188→} 189→

message.content: How do I specify port using Makefile?

content.text: Currently the Makefile hardcodes `make serve`. You can pass the port via a variable:

input.old_string:
serve: build
	moon run backend --target native
input.new_string:
PORT ?= 4000
serve: build
	moon run backend --target native -- --port $(PORT)

.PHONY: build-frontend build-backend build serve clean

build-frontend:

build-backend:

build: build-frontend build-backend

clean:

	moon clean
